title: Level 883

{{DISPLAYTITLE:𝙻𝚎𝚟𝚎𝚕 𝟾𝟾𝟹 "The Pipes..."}} {{Blurbackground|file=Level 883 fondito.png}} {{CSS|content= /* Page */ .page { border: solid 2px rgb(60, 60, 60); margin-top: 20px; position: relative; border-radius: 8px; background-color: rgba(25, 25, 30, 0.95); box-shadow: 4px 4px 30px rgba(20, 20, 25, 0.7); font-family: 'Courier New', Courier, monospace; color: rgb(160, 180, 170); padding: 20px; text-shadow: 2px 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.7); } a { color: #6a9b6a; text-decoration: none; font-weight: bold; text-shadow: 1px 1px 4px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5); } a:hover { color: #5b8a5b; text-shadow: 0px 0px 8px #6a9b6a; } .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(20, 20, 25, 0.9); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(15, 15, 20, 0.8); --theme-link-color: rgb(90, 140, 120); --theme-border-color: rgb(60, 60, 70); .page-side-tool { border: solid 2px rgb(50, 50, 60); box-shadow: 3px 3px 20px rgba(30, 30, 40, 0.6); } --theme-community-header-color: rgb(130, 170, 160); --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: rgba(20, 20, 25, 0.9); --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: rgb(150, 180, 170); --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: rgb(200, 230, 220); } .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(30, 35, 45, 0.9); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(25, 30, 40, 0.8); --theme-link-color: rgb(100, 140, 130); --theme-border-color: rgb(70, 80, 90); .page-side-tool { border: solid 2px rgb(60, 70, 80); box-shadow: 3px 3px 20px rgba(40, 50, 60, 0.6); } --theme-community-header-color: rgb(110, 150, 140); --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: rgba(25, 30, 40, 0.9); --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: rgb(170, 200, 190); --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: rgb(210, 240, 230); } }} {{Class Custom Image|bg-image=Level 883 fondito.png|class=Undetermined|safety=Safety Undetermined|security=Unstable |entity=Presence of Entities Undetermined|hexagon=?|text-color=#ffffff}}
==Description==
'''Level 883''' is an infinitely large sewer network consisting of concrete tubes illuminated by artificial light. The level has a clear demarcation between two regions: the illuminated region and the dark region. The two are not so different, though there is no logical means of determining where one starts and the other ends. =Illuminated Zone= {{Blurimage|file=One of all the sewers.png|caption=One of all the sewers.|hovertext=Wather.|align=Left}} The lit area seems to be intended for individuals to walk through. The corridors are wide, with metal grates above parts of the floor and rusty railings above the waterways. Fluorescent lights are installed on the walls, providing a constant white light that never fades. The walls are clean, with only water and time. The water hardly collects into small spaces that don't go above the ground. It trickles all the time, a rhythm. It's more like a crowded tourist spot than a sewer itself. There are metal placards along the walkways and international danger placards. No smell anywhere. No living things. Only thick quiet, too sterile. As you continue walking, a sense of security is present but unobtrusive. Steps make their normal sound. Everything is. normal. But the longer you move, the more meaningless the branches are creating. Some routes keep circling upon themselves. Some doors padlocked with rusty chains, others plain go nowhere. You never reach there. But then the light runs out of steam. =Dark Zone= The dark zone is another matter. The light just stops, as if it had nothing left to go. There is no running down, but rather an unseen boundary where the lamps do not shine. Here the water is black, dense, ankle-deep and knee-deep without warning. The air is dense, with a slightly sour smell. There are no currents, but the surfaces seem to ripple just out of reach. The dripping is different. Slower. Less predictable. The walls are rough, moss and cracks that never existed in the lit area. No markings. No railings. Only tunnels that fall into the darkness, thinner, deeper. The echoes in here are slow. Sometimes they sound like they're ahead of your own footsteps. Everything is okay at first. The dripping water sound is peaceful. You walk. And walk. And walk. The tunnels continue. You are not weary. You hear nothing approaching. You think — no, you are certain — that you took a detour around this curve. Or hadn't you? {{Blurimage|file=dibujin.png|caption=the drawing of hallucination.|hovertext=ME.}} You glance over your shoulder, and the path down which you were approaching is different. Was this way there previously? You cannot tell. You don't care. You push ahead. In the darkest places, the water is deeper. It's only knee-deep initially, though. There isn't any current. No waves. No movement. You continue. You continue because to stop is worse. To stop is to listen to what the water says. It is to have the drops no longer repeating the same thing. It is to know your footsteps ring. but the echo never with your step. Do not return. The air is thick. The water is thick. The light melts. Or is the water emitting light? It matters not anymore. Nothing is. You have no idea how long you have been here. You have no idea how you got here. You have no idea if ever there was a way of getting here. There is no creature. There is no danger. There is no haven. There is something else. There is something. and it pursues you when your eyes are closed. I will never be able to return [[A.A.E.G.|~ ℳ𝒶𝓉𝒽𝑒𝓌]]

{{Underline|003300}} The above was found on a soggy, crumpled sheet of paper in the sewers' darkest corner, alongside a drawing of "an hallucination." The majority of [[A.A.E.G.]] researchers claim it is merely a hallucination, but this cannot be confirmed, as the level is unstable and not much is known about it. {{Blurimage|file=Hayno.png|caption="a hallucination that whoever drew it calls Norvelquial"|hovertext=Me.|align=Left}} The above is the authoritative description as per the [[A.A.E.G.|Λ.λ.∈.G]], since nothing much is known about the level. The [[A.A.E.G.]] has attempted several times to explore the level, but it is believed to be impossible since the level has a psychological phenomenon known as "Esta," which makes one easily lost. It causes visual, hearing, and touch hallucinations. The "Esta" effect usually makes ramblers see hallucinations with people in many such hallucinations and thus the reason the accompanying drawing to the miniature level description is thought to also be a hallucination. Moreover, the effect also makes victims utter nonsequiturs and blank out in the mind, and it's because description would have things being nonsensical or illogical. It is said that the "Esta" effect causes the person to feel nothing several hours after the initial symptoms begin, leading to fainting. What then happens is not known, but is believed to kill the person. {{Blurimage|file=nos.png|caption="a hallucination that whoever drew it calls Norvelquial"|hovertext=ME.}} The [[A.A.E.G.]] has had numerous tries to find a way to explore, investigate, and locate any object or peculiar anomaly of interest in the level with the hopes of establishing a base of operations. No technique, tool, or body of knowledge can assist in exploration or reverse the "Esta" effect. Any approach attempted did not work, as the psychological effect of the level makes any form of exploration virtually impossible. Since the "Esta" effect is so ephemeral in its nature, height is among the most unmapped in all of the Backrooms. With the inability to find any type of landmark and the hallucinogenic effect the phenomenon causes, it is such that a map of the ground or even a temporary headquarters of any sort cannot be made. The [[A.A.E.G.]] continues to research methods of nullifying the effect or even noticing patterns in the hallucinations, but the pace is slow. Until the issue is corrected, the level remains in a state of unclassification and outposts, its real character held in reserve. ==Colonies & Outposts== Due to the conditions it is impossible to have a base at the level ==Entrances== *in [[Level 33]], among the intriguing facets of the supermarket, a peculiarity is discovered: a unique dust that, when inhaled, induces the ability to noclip, allowing transcendence to Level 883. ==Exits==
[[Level 550|Just wait until you discover it yourself.]]
